model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increase feature hierarchy and moderate capacity within the same training budget by adding a third pooling stage and slightly widening the network, which should improve CIFAR-10 representation quality without introducing complex training changes.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d(1),
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(in_ch, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","old":"        feat_size = IMAGE_SIZE // (2**DEPTH)
        feat_dim = in_ch * feat_size * feat_size
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(feat_dim, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Replace the large flattened head with global average pooling so capacity stays focused in the convolutional trunk, reducing over-parameterization in the classifier and usually improving CIFAR-10 generalization at this budget.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        feat_size = IMAGE_SIZE // (2**DEPTH)
        feat_dim = in_ch * feat_size * feat_size
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(feat_dim, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","old":"        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d(1),
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(in_ch, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Restore the better-performing dense head and add mild label smoothing, which often lowers validation cross-entropy on CIFAR-10 under short budgets by reducing overconfidence without changing the optimization setup.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Slightly widen the three-stage CNN and its classifier head to raise representational capacity while keeping the overall design unchanged, which is a natural next architecture step after the strong gain from adding the third stage.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The widened three-stage CNN may still be slightly under-driven in a 256-step budget, so a moderate learning-rate increase is a simple high-upside follow-up to accelerate early fitting without changing the training recipe.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed step budget, doubling batch size lets each optimizer step incorporate more examples, which can improve gradient quality and effective data coverage without altering the successful model or optimizer recipe.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The large gain from moving to batch size 128 suggests the fixed-step regime strongly rewards more examples per optimizer step, so testing batch size 256 is the highest-upside continuation while keeping the successful architecture and optimizer unchanged.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After the large batch-size gains, the optimizer is likely stable enough to benefit from a modest learning-rate increase, which may improve progress per step without changing the now-strong architecture and batch regime.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strongest improvements have come from increasing examples per optimizer step under the fixed 256-step regime, so testing batch size 512 is the most direct high-upside continuation of the current successful trajectory.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With batch size 512 performing strongly, the current optimizer likely has room for a higher step size; increasing the learning rate is the simplest next move to better exploit the large-batch fixed-step regime without changing the proven architecture.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The search has consistently improved by increasing examples per optimizer step under the fixed-step budget, so moving from batch size 512 to 1024 is the clearest remaining high-upside test while preserving the now-strong optimizer and architecture settings.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The large-batch trajectory keeps improving, and each prior learning-rate increase helped, so the cleanest next step is to raise the learning rate again to better match batch size 1024 without changing the successful architecture or training loop.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The current large-batch, high-learning-rate regime is now quite aggressive and effectively sees many epochs within the fixed step budget, so increasing AdamW weight decay is a simple way to test whether stronger regularization lowers validation loss further.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.02)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"At this stronger large-batch regime, a very small amount of label smoothing is worth testing because it can lower validation cross-entropy by reducing overconfidence without the heavy regularization penalty that came from the earlier 0.1 setting.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.02)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The small label-smoothing test regressed validation loss, so the highest-confidence next step is to remove it and return to the stronger unsmoothed objective before spending the remaining budget on riskier changes.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The clearest successful pattern in this run has been increasing examples per optimizer step under the fixed 256-step budget, so testing batch size 2048 is the highest-upside continuation of the current trajectory while keeping the strong large-batch optimizer settings intact.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Every successful large-batch configuration so far has also benefited from a higher learning rate, so the most direct next step is to scale the step size up again to better match batch size 2048 while preserving the rest of the strong recipe.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 4096","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Across the entire run, the most reliable improvement pattern has been increasing examples per optimizer step under the fixed 256-step budget, so for the final shot the highest-upside move is one more batch-size doubling while keeping the successful optimizer and learning-rate settings unchanged.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}
